title: pi-autoresearch Tour
description: Walk through the extension, skills, and dashboard that power pi's autonomous experiment loop.
prerequisites: Familiarity with pi (pi.dev) extensions and skills

steps.title: Project Overview
steps.description: pi-autoresearch gives pi an autonomous optimize-measure-keep-or-revert loop. The repo splits into a runtime extension, two skills, a dashboard template, and tests.

steps.title: The Extension Runtime
steps.description: A single runtime module is the source of truth for the extension. It registers the experiment tools, renders the live status widget above the editor, and implements the autoresearch command with its on, off, clear, and export modes.

steps.title: Skills: Create & Finalize
steps.description: Two skills bracket the loop. The first bootstraps a session — goal, command, metric, and files in scope — and kicks the loop off. The second takes a noisy experiment branch at the end and splits it into clean, independently reviewable branches.

steps.title: Dashboard & Assets
steps.description: The live dashboard opened by autoresearch export lives here as a templated page that auto-updates in the browser as experiments run, alongside the project logo.

steps.title: Tests
steps.description: An end-to-end test exercises the branch-splitting logic that turns messy experiment history into reviewable pull requests — the critical path for finalizing a session.

steps.title: Configuration & Docs
steps.description: The manifest declares the extension so pi can install it, and the readme walks through the full workflow, keyboard shortcuts, and examples.

steps.title: Full Codebase Recap
steps.description: You've seen the runtime, the skills that bracket each session, the live dashboard, the finalize test, and the project manifest. From here, try the autoresearch command in pi or head back to the readme for install details.
